items.headline: 67-unit mixed-use project proposed near retail shops
items.summary: Sunnyvale Planning Commission is reviewing a 4.22-acre development that would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartments alongside 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story building. The project also requires subdividing the land into 17 lots and would preserve the existing Speedy's Tacos location.

items.headline: Campbell raises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council passed an ordinance revising business license tax rates in Campbell, effective in the upcoming fiscal year. This is an annual update that affects local businesses operating in the city.

items.headline: City addresses affordable housing protections if subsidies expire
items.summary: Council deferred a discussion about what happens to affordability restrictions when project-based housing subsidies end or buildings face foreclosure. This affects long-term stability of affordable units in San José.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves massive stadium signage for tournament
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. This marks a significant visual change to the stadium area during the tournament events.

items.headline: City Council Approves FY 2026-27 Development and User Fee Updates
items.summary: Milpitas updated development fees and user charges for the upcoming fiscal year, which affects costs for residents and businesses seeking permits or city services. These fee adjustments typically impact housing projects, commercial development, and utility expenses.

items.headline: County Homelessness Plan Presented to Milpitas City Council
items.summary: Santa Clara County presented its Community Plan to End Homelessness to the council on April 21, signaling potential new local initiatives or partnerships to address homelessness in Milpitas. This may affect how the city allocates resources or coordinates services.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 brings five new homes to Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Five single-family homes ranging from $487K-$573K are under construction along Altino Boulevard as part of the Comm Hill development's third phase. This represents meaningful new housing supply in San José, with multiple unit types suggesting diversity in the neighborhood.

items.headline: City releases guide to building permits and development timelines
items.summary: San José published a resource explaining the development approval process, from initial concept through occupancy. This could help residents and developers understand how long projects take and what's involved in building in the city.
